segments.text: Follow God's example, therefore, as dearly loved children
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: walk in the way of love, just as Christ loved us and gave himself up for us as a fragrant offering and sacrifice to God
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But among you there must not be even a hint of sexual immorality, or of any kind of impurity, or of greed, because these are improper for God's holy people
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Nor should there be obscenity, foolish talk or coarse joking, which are out of place, but rather thanksgiving
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For of this you can be sure: No immoral, impure or greedy person—such a person is an idolater—has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of such things God's wrath comes on those who are disobedient
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore do not be partners with them
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For you were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. Live as children of light
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: for the fruit of the light consists in all goodness, righteousness and truth
and find out what pleases the Lord
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Have nothing to do with the fruitless deeds of darkness, but rather expose them
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: It is shameful even to mention what the disobedient do in secret
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But everything exposed by the light becomes visible—and everything that is illuminated becomes a light
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Wake up, sleeper, rise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you
segments.speaker: Christ

segments.text: Be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord's will is
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Do not get drunk on wine, which leads to debauchery. Instead, be filled with the Spirit
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: speaking to one another with psalms, hymns, and songs from the Spirit. Sing and make music from your heart to the Lord
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: always giving thanks to God the Father for everything, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Wives, submit yourselves to your own husbands as you do to the Lord
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: to make her holy, cleansing her by the washing with water through the word
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: and to present her to himself as a radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: In this same way, husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: After all, no one ever hated their own body, but they feed and care for their body, just as Christ does the church
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: for we are members of his body
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: This is a profound mystery—but I am talking about Christ and the church
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: However, each one of you also must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband
segments.speaker: Paul
